summary: Frontend-only smoke test of HeroBanner autoplay video rewrite. All 10 acceptance criteria PASSED at both desktop (1920x900) and mobile (390x844). Video autoplays muted/looping inline on Playwright Chromium (using webm source), both /hero_loop.webm and /hero_loop.mp4 return HTTP 200 with correct MIME types, mobile hero fully above the fold (before/after bottom=634px <= 844px viewport, no horizontal overflow), desktop layout has copy on LEFT and video card on RIGHT with before/after below the card, all required data-testids present, old overlays (countdown-badge, viral-caption, bid-toast) fully removed, no console errors, and both CTA buttons navigate correctly (/customer/create and /marketplace).
critical_code_review_comments: HeroBanner.jsx (143 lines) is clean and focused — implementation is solid.
Video tag correctly has autoPlay/loop/muted/playsInline plus a defensive useEffect that re-plays on canplay, which is the right belt-and-suspenders pattern for autoplay reliability across browsers.
webm listed before mp4 as expected — Chromium picks VP9 (~730KB) over the 2.7MB H.264 fallback, saves bandwidth for most users while still serving Safari/iOS via mp4.
context_for_next_testing_agent: HeroBanner video hero is verified working at both viewports. If regressions happen later, the key assertions to re-run are: (1) video.paused===false && video.currentTime>0 within ~5s of load, (2) at 390x844 the [data-testid=hero-before-after].getBoundingClientRect().bottom must be <= 844, (3) currentSrc should end with .webm on Chromium. Both video files are served correctly from /app/frontend/public/. No backend involvement — this hero is 100% static.
